pattern: Stacking boxes into a corner pyramid greedily: fill complete triangular layers first, then add floor boxes one at a time, each enabling a chain of stacked boxes.
hints: A perfect corner pyramid of h levels holds h(h+1)(h+2)/6 boxes and touches h(h+1)/2 on the floor.
Use as many complete pyramid levels as fit, then place extra floor boxes; the j-th extra floor box supports j additional stacked boxes.
After the largest full pyramid, greedily add floor boxes increasing the supported count 1,2,3,... until the remaining boxes are covered.

edge_inputs.description: exactly a 2-level base needs 3 on floor
one more than a full small pyramid
exact full pyramid of three levels

stress.description: random large n across the range

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minimumBoxes(self, n):
        total = 0
        floor = 0
        height = 0
        # add complete triangular layers while they fit
        layer = 0
        while True:
            layer += 1
            layer_size = layer * (layer + 1) // 2
            if total + layer_size > n:
                layer -= 1
                break
            total += layer_size
            floor += layer
        # add floor boxes one at a time; the j-th new floor box supports j boxes
        j = 0
        while total < n:
            j += 1
            total += j
            floor += 1
        return floor

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minimumBoxes(self, n):
        total = 0
        floor = 0
        k = 0
        while total + (k + 1) * (k + 2) // 2 <= n:
            k += 1
            total += k * (k + 1) // 2
            floor += k
        j = 0
        while total < n:
            j += 1
            total += j
            floor += 1
        return floor
